title: TLS, Virtual Hosts, Credentials, and Authorization · Language Lens: Amqps Identity Verification
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must verify server identity, roots, client credentials, vhost permissions, rotation, redaction, and tenant abuse boundaries; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the amqps identity verification increment in a Go TLS and least-privilege connection contract. Learner…
objectives: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
